purpose: Monitoring companion app for DENG Tool: Rejoin
changelog: Snapshot finally works on rooted cloud phones: the Termux bridge now walks a real capture ladder — normal screencap, /system/bin/screencap, root screencap to stdout, and (the reliable one) root 'screencap -p /sdcard/…' read back from file — automatically escalating to root when the unprivileged path is blocked. Root is on by default; set DENG_REJOIN_SNAPSHOT_USE_SU=0 to disable.

Every capture attempt is now validated as a real PNG (89 50 4E 47 …) before upload. Empty output, shell-error text, and non-PNG bytes are rejected and the next provider is tried, so a 0-byte 'image' is never uploaded.

Honest snapshot diagnostics: the bridge records which provider was attempted/selected, su availability, root grant, byte length, PNG validity, and a precise result (success / failed_no_screencap / failed_root_denied / failed_invalid_png / failed_timeout / failed_upload_http). The Snapshot screen shows a clean reason + Retry instead of an infinite spinner, and the probe gains a SNAPSHOT PROOF section.

Snapshot upload is isolated from the heartbeat: an upload failure (HTTP error, oversized image) never breaks the device's online status. Heartbeat controls connection; snapshot only controls snapshot status.

Redesigned, device-centric Dashboard: a compact header with Last Update + Interval + sync indicator, a TOTAL / ONLINE / DEAD / RAM stats row, and a per-device RAM list ('2048MB/4096MB 50% - Cloud Phone 1', tap to expand). Device counts are TTL-based and never affected by snapshot failures. RAM is read from /proc/meminfo (root-free) and never invented.
Backend returns dashboard-friendly fields on /api/monitor/devices (computed connection_state, per-device device_ram, snapshot_last_result) while staying backward compatible with older app versions.
